name: Great White Shark
summary: The great white shark, also known as the white shark, white pointer, or great white, is a large shark. It is closely related to the mako sharks, the porbeagle, and the salmon shark. It is a robustly built species with a grayish upperside and a white underside. The white shark is one of the largest living shark and fish species, being smaller than the whale shark and basking shark. It has about 300 triangular, serrated teeth that are continuously replaced. Its massive, fatty liver can reach over a quarter of its body weight, providing buoyancy and storing energy. The species is partially warm-blooded, an adaptation that allows it to remain active in colder waters.

wikipedia_sections.etymology: carcharias Binomial name Carcharodon carcharias ( Linnaeus , 1758 ) Range based on Huveneers et al. It is closely related to the mako sharks , the porbeagle , and the salmon shark . It is a robustly built species with a grayish upperside and a white underside. Etymology and naming The name 'great white shark' likely comes from the white underside exposed on beached sharks. The most common English names for the species include great white shark, white shark, and Australian variant white pointer. 6 7 These names refer to its white underside, which is noticeable in dead sharks lying upside down.
wikipedia_sections.description: For the novel, see White Shark (novel) . The white shark is one of the largest living shark and fish species, being smaller than the whale shark and basking shark . It has about 300 triangular, serrated teeth that are continuously replaced. Its massive, fatty liver can reach over a quarter of its body weight, providing buoyancy and storing energy. 10 Phylogeny Most phylogenetic analyses based on molecular data or anatomical features place the white shark as the sister clade to the mako shark clade with the Lamna clade as the sister clade to all others in the family.
wikipedia_sections.distribution: Great white shark Temporal range: Early Pliocene – Recent 5–0 Ma 1 PreꞒ Ꞓ O S D C P T J K Pg N Pal. Populations are most concentrated at the Pacific and Atlantic sides of North America and in the waters of southern Africa and Oceania . They are a highly migratory species, traveling between the coast and open ocean and even between continents. c 18 A more recent 2024 clock shifted the range between 57.2 and 31.8 mya. d 19 A minority of analyses recovered an alternate placement of the white shark as the sister clade to the rest.
wikipedia_sections.habitat: White sharks inhabit tropical and temperate ocean waters around the world and can be found both along the coast and further out to sea. 30 Instead, populations within the major ocean basins show distinct genetic lineages that diverged from each other at least thousands of years ago. 20 The datings by the 2024 study coincide with the penultimate glaciation , which may have restricted cross-oceanic movements through sea level fall. 68 Distribution and habitat Shark being baited in Guadalupe Island Biosphere Reserve , Mexico White sharks range from tropical to temperate and even colder waters aro
wikipedia_sections.hunting_diet: The white shark preys on marine mammals such as seals and dolphins , as well as fish, including other sharks, and cephalopods . It is also a prolific scavenger of whale carcasses. Though normally an apex predator , the species is sometimes preyed on by orcas . White sharks are generally solitary, but may gather in aggregations, particularly at feeding sites. They may communicate and establish dominance hierarchies with body language . Juvenile white sharks typically inhabit shallower water and are limited to feeding on smaller prey.
wikipedia_sections.behavior: The species is partially warm-blooded , an adaptation that allows it to remain active in colder waters. 77 Behavior and ecology White shark surfacing at Dyer Island White sharks are estimated to swim 2.88–4.86 km/h (1.79–3.02 mph), 83 but can sprint up to 24.12 km/h (14.99 mph). 84 f One individual was recorded cruising at a sustained speed of 4.7 km/h (2.9 mph) while migrating, which is fast for a shark and more similar to fast-swimming tuna . 76 White sharks display various surface behaviors. 88 89 The white shark is generally considered to be a solitary species, though aggregations do occur
wikipedia_sections.reproduction: The species reproduces with pups hatching from eggs inside the female before being born live. 64 The lipids and fatty acids provide the shark energy for travel and also power reproduction and growth. 62 63 One study concluded that a white shark liver is more energy-rich than whale blubber . 63 White sharks appear to have strong immune systems and can tolerate high amounts of toxic heavy metals like mercury and arsenic in their blood, more so than other vertebrates. 65 They are also documented to heal relatively quickly from even severe wounds, 66 and the species' genome shows "positive selecti
wikipedia_sections.threats: 154 The novel and film helped create the image of the species as a dangerous man-eater. Benchley would later express regret stating "I cannot rewrite Jaws , nor make an ignoble monster of this magnificent animal." 155 Compared to other fish, the white shark was not an important species for fishermen. Their meat was considered tasty but was not considered worth it due to the difficulty of hauling them in. 187 The results of this study are disputed; in 2024 it was noted that catches of white sharks in KZN have declined since 2010, suggesting they have not moved eastward.
wikipedia_sections.conservation: Miocene P P Male off Isla Guadalupe , Mexico Conservation status Vulnerable ( 3.1 ) 2 (Global) Critically Endangered ( 3.1 ) 3 4 (Europe and the Mediterranean) Scientific classification Kingdom: Animalia Phylum: Chordata Class: Chondrichthyes Subclass: Elasmobranchii Division: Selachii Order: Lamniformes Family: Lamnidae Genus: Carcharodon Smith , 1838 Species: C. The International Union for Conservation of Nature lists the white shark as a vulnerable species globally and critically endangered regionally in European and Mediterranean waters.

ecology.habitat: Temperate, coastal waters and open ocean
